title: Administration Executive
job_description:
Summary of The Role The Administration Executive plays a key role in supporting the delivery of assessment services by ensuring a seamless and well-coordinated client journey. This position is responsible for managing client communications, coordinating assessment schedules, and maintaining accurate administrative processes across the service. The role requires strong organisational skills, attention to detail, and the ability to manage multiple tasks in a fast-paced environment. Working closely with clients, clinical professionals, and internal stakeholders, the Administration Executive ensures a high standard of customer service, efficient coordination, and effective resolution of queries. Operating with a high level of ownership, the role requires a proactive approach, strong communication skills, and a commitment to delivering a supportive and high-quality client experience. Role Responsibilities
  • Acting as the first point of contact for client enquiries via phone and email, ensuring a professional, empathetic, and supportive approach.
  • Providing clear and detailed information on the assessment process, addressing client queries effectively.
  • Following up on enquiries in a timely manner to ensure clients remain informed and supported.
  • Coordinating and scheduling assessment appointments between clients and clinical associates.
  • Liaising with clinical teams, associates, and internal stakeholders to ensure smooth service delivery.
  • Managing and maintaining accurate client records across internal systems.
  • Navigating IT systems efficiently to support administrative and operational processes.
  • Booking and coordinating logistics, including Hub rooms and assessment resources.
  • Coordinating and distributing assessment kits to clients as required.
  • Handling queries from associates and resolving issues promptly and professionally.
  • Identifying and escalating potential risks that may impact the client journey.
  • Supporting continuous improvement of processes to enhance service delivery.
  • Building strong working relationships across teams to ensure a cohesive and efficient service.
  • Assisting with general administrative duties to support the wider team.
job_requirements:
Essential Skills, Knowledge & Experience
  • Previous experience in an administration, coordination, or customer-facing role.
  • Strong organisational skills with the ability to manage multiple tasks and priorities.
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
  • High attention to detail and accuracy.
  • Strong customer service skills with the ability to handle sensitive situations with empathy.
  • Experience working with multiple stakeholders and managing relationships.
  • Proficient in IT systems and comfortable managing data and schedules.
  • Ability to work independently and take ownership of responsibilities.
 Personal Attributes 
  • Must be accountable with a strong work ethic, a sense of urgency and ownership, and be able to work effectively both independently and as part of a team.
  • Data driven, highly organized, and detailed oriented.
  • Able to deliver as efficiently as possible without compromising quality or the customer experience.
  • First class communicator with an ability to engage sales teams, operational teams and customer stakeholders up to a senior level with confidence.
  • Efficient in approach, developing optimized and simple working methods and processes with a keen eye for detail.
  • Continually improving, caring about quality, and delivering customer value.
job_benefits:
Work Environment 
  • General Work Hours: 1.30PM – 10.30PM (WFH) Sri Lanka Time – 45hrs/week.
  • Location: Remote WFH + Occasional Meets at our Colombo 5 office – Sri Lanka.
  • Benefits: Paid leave, career upskilling opportunities, eLearning courses.

title: HR and Payroll Executive
job_description:
Summary of The Role The HR & Payroll Executive plays a key role in supporting the day-to-day HR operations and ensuring accurate and timely payroll processing. This position is responsible for maintaining employee records, supporting recruitment and onboarding activities, and managing payroll administration across the organization. The role requires strong attention to detail, excellent organizational skills, and the ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment. Working closely with HR leadership, managers, and administrators, the HR & Payroll Executive ensures compliance with employment regulations and internal policies while maintaining high standards of accuracy and confidentiality. Operating with a high level of ownership, the role requires a proactive approach, strong communication skills, and the ability to support both HR and payroll functions effectively. Role Responsibilities
  • Providing day-to-day administrative support to the HR function.
  • Maintaining and updating employee records within the HR system (HRIS).
  • Supporting recruitment activities, including posting job adverts, screening CVs, scheduling interviews, and onboarding.
  • Preparing employment contracts, letters, and HR documentation as required.
  • Managing the HR inbox and responding to employee queries in a timely and professional manner.
  • Supporting absence management processes and maintaining accurate records.
  • Coordinating induction activities and supporting onboarding processes.
  • Ensuring compliance with employment legislation and internal policies.
  • Supporting training administration, including maintaining training records and coordinating courses.
  • Preparing HR reports and supporting audits and compliance checks.
  • Maintaining accurate and up-to-date personnel files and documentation.
  • Assisting with HR meetings, including note-taking for investigations or disciplinary processes.
  • Supporting general HR administration and ad hoc tasks as required.
Payroll Responsibilities
  • Supporting the end-to-end payroll process, ensuring accuracy and adherence to deadlines.
  • Reviewing and validating timesheets, rotas, and attendance data prior to payroll submission.
  • Preparing payroll input files and coordinating with payroll providers or internal teams.
  • Processing payroll-related changes, including new starters, leavers, and contract variations.
  • Ensuring accurate calculation of variable pay elements, including overtime and allowances.
  • Supporting statutory payroll requirements (e.g., SSP, SMP, deductions such as student loans).
  • Maintaining payroll records and resolving payroll-related queries.
job_requirements:
Essential Skills, Knowledge & Experience
  • Previous experience in an HR Administration and/or Payroll role (2–3 years preferred).
  • Strong understanding of HR processes and payroll administration.
  • Experience working with HR systems (e.g., Planday or similar HRIS platforms).
  • Proficient in MS Office applications, particularly Excel (pivot tables, formulas).
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
  • Strong organizational skills with the ability to manage multiple tasks and deadlines.
  • High attention to detail and accuracy, particularly when handling sensitive data.
  • Understanding of confidentiality, data protection, and compliance requirements.
  • Ability to work independently and as part of a team.
Desirable:
  • HR or business-related qualification (e.g., CIPD Level 3 or equivalent).
  • Experience working with UK HR and payroll processes.
  • Exposure to compliance within regulated environments (e.g., healthcare or care sector).
Personal Attributes 
  • Must be accountable with a strong work ethic, a sense of urgency and ownership, and be able to work effectively both independently and as part of a team.
  • Data driven, highly organized, and detailed oriented.
  • Able to deliver as efficiently as possible without compromising quality or the customer experience.
  • First class communicator with an ability to engage sales teams, operational teams and customer stakeholders up to a senior level with confidence.
  • Efficient in approach, developing optimized and simple working methods and processes with a keen eye for detail.
  • Continually improving, caring about quality, and delivering customer value.
job_benefits:
Work Environment 
  • General Work Hours: 1.30PM – 10.30PM (WFH) Sri Lanka Time – 45hrs/week.
  • Location: Remote WFH + Occasional Meets at our Colombo 5 office – Sri Lanka.
  • Benefits: Paid leave, career upskilling opportunities, eLearning courses.

title: Operations Executive
job_description:
Summary of The Role The Operations Administrator plays a key role in ensuring the seamless coordination and delivery of training services. This position is responsible for supporting the day-to-day operations of the training function, maintaining high standards of customer experience, and ensuring operational efficiency across all activities. The role requires strong organisational skills, attention to detail, and the ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast-paced environment. Acting as a central point of contact for customers, trainers, and stakeholders, the Training Coordinator ensures smooth delivery and high-quality service at all times. Working with minimal supervision, the role requires a proactive approach, strong communication skills, and a commitment to continuous improvement. Role Responsibilities Training Operations & Coordination
  • Act as the central point of contact for all customers, responding promptly, professionally, and effectively to enquiries.
  • Build strong, collaborative relationships with trainers, associates, partners, and clients.
  • Provide end-to-end administrative support across all training delivery (pre- and post-course), including scheduling, online facilitation, and customer support.
  • Maintain accurate and up-to-date documentation, including training calendars and learning data.
Service Management & Stakeholder Engagement
  • Work closely with internal teams and external stakeholders to ensure effective service delivery.
  • Interpret contractual requirements to ensure delivery aligns with agreed terms and resolve client queries.
  • Collect and analyse client feedback, supporting evaluation processes and performance tracking.
  • Engage with customers to review services, identify improvements, and resolve issues.
  • Collaborate with marketing to ensure courses are effectively promoted and communicated.
  • Coordinate with finance to ensure timely invoicing and payment processing.
  • Support the recruitment and onboarding of trainers and consultants, including contract updates.
  • Ensure all course materials remain current, relevant, and of high quality.
Team Contribution
  • Priorities workload effectively and continuously seek ways to improve service delivery.
  • Step in to resolve operational issues in the absence of other team members when required.
  • Undertake additional responsibilities aligned with the needs of the service and individual capabilities.
job_requirements:
Essential Skills, Knowledge & Experience
  • Previous experience in a coordination, administration, or operations role.
  • Strong organizational skills with the ability to manage multiple tasks and deadlines.
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
  • High attention to detail and accuracy.
  • Experience working with multiple stakeholders and managing relationships.
  • Proficient in MS Office and comfortable using systems to manage data and schedules.
  • Ability to work independently and take ownership of tasks.
 Personal Attributes 
  • Must be accountable with a strong work ethic, a sense of urgency and ownership, and be able to work effectively both independently and as part of a team.
  • Data driven, highly organized, and detailed oriented.
  • Able to deliver as efficiently as possible without compromising quality or the customer experience.
  • First class communicator with an ability to engage sales teams, operational teams and customer stakeholders up to a senior level with confidence.
  • Efficient in approach, developing optimized and simple working methods and processes with a keen eye for detail.
  • Continually improving, caring about quality, and delivering customer value.
job_benefits:
Work Environment 
  • General Work Hours: 1.30PM – 10.30PM (WFH) Sri Lanka Time – 45hrs/week.
  • Location: Remote WFH + Occasional Meets at our Colombo 5 office – Sri Lanka.
  • Benefits: Paid leave, career upskilling opportunities, eLearning courses.

title: Finance Executive
job_description:
Summary of The Role The Finance Executive (Accounts Payable) plays a key role in supporting the finance function by ensuring accurate and timely processing of supplier invoices and payments. This position is responsible for maintaining the purchase ledger, reconciling supplier accounts, and supporting day-to-day accounts payable operations. The role requires strong attention to detail, organizational skills, and the ability to manage multiple tasks in a fast-paced environment. Working closely with suppliers and internal stakeholders, the Finance Executive ensures accurate financial records, timely payments, and effective resolution of queries. Operating with a high level of ownership, the role requires a proactive approach, strong communication skills, and the ability to meet strict monthly deadlines. Role Responsibilities
  • Processing supplier invoices, matching delivery notes to invoices and purchase orders.
  • Reconciling supplier statements on a monthly basis and resolving discrepancies.
  • Setting up new supplier accounts and maintaining accurate supplier records.
  • Preparing purchase summaries and supporting reporting requirements.
  • Processing employee expense claims and ensuring compliance with company policies.
  • Managing cashbook entries, petty cash, and cheque processing.
  • Preparing and processing payment runs, including BACS payments.
  • Handling supplier queries via email and phone, ensuring timely resolution.
  • Maintaining accurate and up-to-date financial records within the purchase ledger.
  • Supporting month-end activities and ensuring all deadlines are met.
  • Assisting with general finance administration and supporting the wider finance team.
job_requirements:
Essential Skills, Knowledge & Experience
  • Previous experience in a Purchase Ledger, Accounts Payable, or Finance Assistant role.
  • Strong understanding of accounts payable processes, including invoice processing and reconciliations.
  • Proficient in MS Excel and familiar with accounting systems (e.g., Sage, SAP, or similar).
  • Experience with payment processing methods such as BACS is advantageous.
  • High attention to detail and accuracy in financial data handling.
  • Strong organizational skills with the ability to manage multiple tasks and deadlines.
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
  • Ability to work independently and as part of a team.
job_benefits:
Work Environment 
  • General Work Hours: 1.30PM – 10.30PM (WFH) Sri Lanka Time – 45hrs/week.
  • Location: Remote WFH + Occasional Meets at our Colombo 5 office – Sri Lanka.
  • Benefits: Paid leave, career upskilling opportunities, eLearning courses.

title: Customer Support Executive
job_description:
Summary of The Role This role supports the overnight operations function for a UK-based private healthcare service by providing frontline patient support and completing key administrative tasks to ensure seamless continuity between overnight and daytime teams. The Customer Support Executive will manage calls and requests, coordinate appointments, update systems, and maintain accurate records while escalating complex or high-risk situations to the Team Leader. The role requires strong communication skills, attention to detail, and a calm, patient-focused approach. Role Responsibilities
  • Respond to calls, messages, and requests from patients and members, escalating complex or urgent issues to the Team Leader when required
  • Handle calls and messages from private patients, members, and corporate clients with empathy and a solution-focused mindset
  • Go beyond being an order-taking service by understanding the patient’s situation, thinking creatively, and applying sound judgement to reach the best possible outcome
  • Manage requests that may be unusual, complex, or non-protocolized. For example, assisting with urgent but non-emergency issues, rerouting attending doctors, or coordinating across regional GP teams
  • Assist with appointment scheduling and coordination with duty doctors as directed by the Team Leader
  • Work collaboratively with the overnight team to ensure timely response during peak periods
  • Anticipate patient needs to resolve queries that may involve complex or unusual situations
  • Maintain accurate notes and logs to support the Team Leader’s handover
Administration and Support Tasks (Overnight Period) Once peak call activity reduces (typically after 10:00 pm UK time), the role will include a range of administrative tasks supporting UK operations. These may include:
  • Update doctor rotas as instructed
  • Record membership payment issues and update systems
  • Prepare referral and follow-up documentation
  • Update patient databases and logs accurately
  • Support onboarding and training coordination activities
  • Assist with quality assurance and feedback data collection
job_requirements:
Essential Skills, Knowledge & Experience
  • Excellent written and spoken English
  • Strong customer service or healthcare coordination experience
  • Calm, confident, and empathetic communication style
  • Strong organizational skills with the ability to multitask and priorities
  • Comfortable working independently and liaising with team members and senior UK colleagues remotely
Personal Attributes 
  • Must be accountable with a strong work ethic, a sense of urgency and ownership, and be able to work effectively both independently and as part of a team.
  • Data driven, highly organized, and detailed oriented.
  • Able to deliver as efficiently as possible without compromising quality or the customer experience.
  • First class communicator with an ability to engage sales teams, operational teams and customer stakeholders up to a senior level with confidence.
  • Efficient in approach, developing optimized and simple working methods and processes with a keen eye for detail.
  • Continually improving, caring about quality, and delivering customer value.
job_benefits:
Work Environment 
  • General Work Hours: 7PM to 7AM UK time - 45h work week (Roster Based)
  • Location: Remote WFH + Occasional Meets at our Colombo 5 office – Sri Lanka.
  • Benefits: Paid leave, career upskilling opportunities, eLearning courses.
